filter_pushdown.note: lineitem sorted ascending by l_shipdate; zone map prunes trailing ~21 blocks where min_val > 10471; binary search on zone map to find block cutoff

data_structures.aggregation_note: 4 groups max: (rflag_code in {0,1,2}) × (lstatus_code in {0,1}). Index = rflag_code*2 + lstatus_code. Flat struct array of {sum_qty, sum_price, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, sum_disc, count} per group. Thread-local copies avoid all contention.
data_structures.zone_map_note: Load 7204-byte zone map file; binary search on block max_val to find first block where max_val > 10471; scan rows [0, cutoff_block_end). Last partial block uses zone.row_count.

parallelism.morsel_note: Each morsel = one zone-map block (100K rows). Assign blocks to threads dynamically. ~579 blocks total after zone-map prune. Each thread maintains its own 4-entry accumulator array.
parallelism.synchronization_note: No shared mutable state during scan. Each thread accumulates into local agg[4]. Sequential O(4×64) merge after parallel phase — negligible cost.

index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks, then num_blocks × {int32_t min_val, int32_t max_val, uint32_t row_count}

physical_plan.scan_note: mmap all 7 columns (l_shipdate int32, l_quantity double, l_extendedprice double, l_discount double, l_tax double, l_returnflag uint8, l_linestatus uint8). Single loop: load shipdate, check filter, if pass load remaining 6 columns and accumulate. Access pattern is fully sequential — maximizes hardware prefetcher effectiveness.
physical_plan.output_sort_note: Only 4 result rows; sort by (rflag_code ASC, lstatus_code ASC) — O(1) since group keys are dense integers 0-3. Decode uint8 codes via dict at output time only.

estimated_cost.notes: 59.99M rows × 7 cols; after zone-map prune ~57.9M rows × 7 cols ≈ 2.3 GB. With 376 GB RAM data likely warm in page cache. 64 threads streaming sequential reads saturate memory bandwidth. AVG computed as SUM/count post-merge — no running mean needed.
